17	// Package controller â dispatch_helpers.go consolidates the three planner
18	// dispatch helpers that all three up-stack reconcilers (Milestone, Phase,
19	// Plan) share (Phase 3 D-A1 / D-A2 / D-A4). The helpers exist to keep the
20	// reconciler bodies from drifting in lockstep â each reconciler is ~80-150
21	// LOC of NEW code instead of ~230 LOC because the shared bits live here.
22	//
23	//   - ResolveProvider walks the Project.Spec.Subagent precedence chain
24	//     per D-C2: levels.{level}.{model,params} â Project default â
25	//     Helm-chart default.
26	//
27	//   - BuildPlannerEnvelope mirrors task_controller.go buildEnvelopeIn for
28	//     planner-level dispatch â sets Role="planner", Level=<level>,
29	//     populates Provider via ResolveProvider, and marshals to []byte.
30	//
31	//   - MaterializeChildCRDs server-side-creates child CRDs from
32	//     EnvelopeOut.ChildCRDs. Enforces the Kind allowlist
33	//     (T-308 mitigation): only {Milestone, Phase, Plan, Task, Wave} pass.
34	//     Each child gets a controller-style owner ref pointing at the parent.
35	//     AlreadyExists is treated as idempotent success (mirrors Phase 2
36	//     SUB-03 / Pitfall F).
37	package controller
38	
39	import (
40		"context"
41		"encoding/json"
42		"fmt"
43		"maps"
44	
45		batchv1 "k8s.io/api/batch/v1"
46		apierrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors"
47		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
48		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
49		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"
50		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
51		logf "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log"
52	
53		tideprojectv1alpha1 "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/api/v1alpha1"
54		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/reporter"
55		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
56	)
57	
58	// spawnReporterIfNeeded idempotently spawns the tide-reporter reader Job for a
59	// planner-level Job completion (Option C / T-09-13: AlreadyExists on Create is
60	// success; a pre-existing reporter Job means this completion was already
61	// observed). Returns isFirstCompletion=true when this is the initial
62	// observation of the planner Job's terminal state: either the reporter Job was
63	// newly spawned, or no ReporterImage is configured at all (stub/test path).
64	// When ReporterImage is set but the parent Project is unresolved, no spawn
65	// happens and isFirstCompletion stays false (mirrors the prior inline blocks
66	// in milestone_controller.go / phase_controller.go).
67	func spawnReporterIfNeeded(
68		ctx context.Context,
69		c client.Client,
70		scheme *runtime.Scheme,
71		parent metav1.Object,
72		project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project,
73		parentKind string,
74		reporterImage string,
75	) (bool, error) {
76		logger := logf.FromContext(ctx)
77		if reporterImage == "" {
78			logger.V(1).Info("skipping reporter Job spawn: ReporterImage not configured",
79				"parentKind", parentKind, "parent", parent.GetName())
80			return true, nil
81		}
82		if project == nil {
83			return false, nil
84		}
85		reporterJobName := fmt.Sprintf("tide-reporter-%s", parent.GetUID())
86		var existing batchv1.Job
87		if gErr := c.Get(ctx, types.NamespacedName{Name: reporterJobName, Namespace: parent.GetNamespace()}, &existing); gErr != nil {
88			if !apierrors.IsNotFound(gErr) {
89				return false, fmt.Errorf("get reporter job %s: %w", reporterJobName, gErr)
90			}
91			// Not found — spawn it (first completion observation).
92			reporterJob := BuildReporterJob(parent, project, defaultSharedPVCName, string(parent.GetUID()), parentKind,
93				ReporterOptions{ReporterImage: reporterImage}, scheme)
94			if cErr := c.Create(ctx, reporterJob); cErr != nil {
95				if !apierrors.IsAlreadyExists(cErr) {
96					return false, fmt.Errorf("create reporter job %s: %w", reporterJobName, cErr)
97				}
98			} else {
99				logger.Info("spawned reporter Job", "job", reporterJobName,
100					"parentKind", parentKind, "parent", parent.GetName())
101			}
102			return true, nil
103		}
104		logger.V(1).Info("reporter Job already exists; skipping spawn (T-09-13)", "job", reporterJobName)
105		return false, nil
106	}
107	
108	// ProviderDefaults carries the Helm-chart-supplied defaults used as the
109	// last fallback in ResolveProvider's precedence chain (D-C2).
110	//
111	// Image is the default subagent image ref (vendor selection). Models maps
112	// level name ("milestone"|"phase"|"plan"|"task") to the per-level default
113	// model identifier. Both are filled at Manager startup from Helm values.
114	type ProviderDefaults struct {
115		// Image is the default subagent image ref. Empty string means
116		// "no Helm default" â caller is responsible for surfacing this
117		// at Job creation time (a missing image is a config error).
118		Image string
119	
120		// Models maps levelâmodel. Missing key means "no Helm default for
121		// that level".
122		Models map[string]string
123	}
124	
125	// ResolveProvider walks Project.Spec.Subagent precedence chain for the
126	// given level (D-C2). Returns a ProviderSpec with Vendor pinned to
127	// "anthropic" in v1.0 (per-vendor selection deferred â CONTEXT.md
128	// "Deferred Ideas"). Model and Params resolve via:
129	//
130	//	project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.<level>.Model â
131	//	project.Spec.Subagent.Model â
132	//	helmDefaults.Models[<level>] â
133	//	"" (caller surfaces missing-config error)
134	//
135	// Params merge: level Params copied first, then Project-level Params
136	// inserted only for keys NOT already set at the level â i.e., level
137	// wins on key conflict.
138	func ResolveProvider(project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project, level string, helmDefaults ProviderDefaults) pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec {
139		// Helper to read per-level overrides.
140		var levelCfg *tideprojectv1alpha1.LevelConfig
141		if project != nil {
142			switch level {
143			case "milestone":
144				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Milestone
145			case "phase":
146				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Phase
147			case "plan":
148				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Plan
149			case "task":
150				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Task
151			}
152		}
153	
154		// Resolve Model.
155		var model string
156		switch {
157		case levelCfg != nil && levelCfg.Model != "":
158			model = levelCfg.Model
159		case project != nil && project.Spec.Subagent.Model != "":
160			model = project.Spec.Subagent.Model
161		default:
162			if helmDefaults.Models != nil {
163				model = helmDefaults.Models[level]
164			}
165		}
166	
167		// Merge Params â level overrides Project defaults on key conflict.
168		var params map[string]string
169		if levelCfg != nil && len(levelCfg.Params) > 0 {
170			params = make(map[string]string, len(levelCfg.Params))
171			maps.Copy(params, levelCfg.Params)
172		}
173		// (Project-level Params are not currently exposed on SubagentConfig
174		// â LevelConfig.Params is the per-level extension; if a future
175		// schema bump adds a top-level Subagent.Params, merge here with
176		// "level wins on conflict" semantics.)
177	
178		return pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec{
179			Vendor: "anthropic",
180			Model:  model,
181			Params: params,
182		}
183	}
184	
185	// BuildPlannerEnvelope constructs and marshals an EnvelopeIn for a
186	// planner-level dispatch. Mirrors task_controller.go:buildEnvelopeIn
187	// (Phase 2) but at the planner level: sets Role="planner",
188	// Level=<level>, populates Provider via ResolveProvider, and reuses the
189	// Caps / ProxyEndpoint / SignedToken parameters supplied by the caller.
190	//
191	// parent is the up-stack CRD whose UID stamps EnvelopeIn.TaskUID (the
192	// field is named TaskUID for backward-compat with Phase 2's envelope
193	// schema, but it carries the parent's UID at the planner level â the
194	// semantic is "the dispatch this envelope drives" regardless of level).
195	//
196	// prompt is the level-appropriate prompt body the dispatching reconciler
197	// supplies; it is assigned verbatim to EnvelopeIn.Prompt so the real
198	// subagent's template render has the actual outcome to author against
199	// (defect #4: prior to this the field was never set and the real Claude
200	// planner saw an empty prompt). token and prompt are DISTINCT params â
201	// token is the credproxy HMAC, prompt is the authoring instruction. The
202	// project planner passes Project.Spec.OutcomePrompt; deeper planners pass
203	// the same outcome (the parent artifact context â MILESTONE.md, the phase
204	// brief, PLAN.md â lives on the PVC and the template instructs reading it,
205	// and ParentName flows through EnvelopeIn.Dispatch).
206	// outcomePromptOf returns project.Spec.OutcomePrompt, nil-safe: the deeper
207	// reconcilers resolve the owning Project by walking the parent chain
208	// (resolveProject / resolveProjectForPlan), which returns nil on a not-yet-
209	// visible chain. A nil project yields "" — the same empty-prompt shape the
210	// real subagent already guards (EMPTY_PROMPT warning) rather than a panic.
211	func outcomePromptOf(project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project) string {
212		if project == nil {
213			return ""
214		}
215		return project.Spec.OutcomePrompt
216	}
217	
218	func BuildPlannerEnvelope(level string, parent metav1.Object, project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project, attempt int, token, prompt string, caps pkgdispatch.Caps, proxyEndpoint string, helmDefaults ProviderDefaults) (pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn, []byte, error) {
219		envIn := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{
220			APIVersion:    pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
221			Kind:          pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeIn,
222			TaskUID:       string(parent.GetUID()),
223			Role:          "planner",
224			Level:         level,
225			Prompt:        prompt,
226			Caps:          caps,
227			ProxyEndpoint: proxyEndpoint,
228			SignedToken:   token,
229			Provider:      ResolveProvider(project, level, helmDefaults),
230		}
231	
232		// Stamp parentName into the dedicated Dispatch metadata field so the stub
233		// planner can populate the child *Ref field (e.g. milestoneRef, phaseRef)
234		// without querying the K8s API â parent.GetName() is the authoritative
235		// source (T-07-03-03: parentName is metadata, not a secret). This is kept
236		// out of Provider.Params (model-parameters-only) so the anthropic runner's
237		// strict param allow-list is not tripped by dispatch metadata.
238		envIn.Dispatch = &pkgdispatch.DispatchMeta{ParentName: parent.GetName()}
239	
240		data, err := json.Marshal(envIn)
241		if err != nil {
242			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal planner envelope: %w", err)
243		}
244		return envIn, data, nil
245	}
246	
247	// MaterializeChildCRDs delegates to reporter.MaterializeChildCRDs.
248	// The materialization machinery lives in internal/reporter so cmd/tide-reporter
249	// can import it without a back-edge to internal/controller (plan 09-04).
250	func MaterializeChildCRDs(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, scheme *runtime.Scheme, parent metav1.Object, children []pkgdispatch.ChildCRDSpec) error {
251		return reporter.MaterializeChildCRDs(ctx, c, scheme, parent, children)
252	}
253	
254	// resolveImage walks Project.Spec.Subagent precedence chain for the given
255	// level, returning the resolved subagent container image reference.
256	//
257	//	Levels.<level>.Image → Spec.Subagent.Image → helmDefaults.Image → ""
258	//
259	// An empty return means no image was configured; callers must surface this
260	// as a config error rather than dispatching a Job with an empty image field.
261	// Level "project" has no entry in the switch (the CRD has no Levels.Project);
262	// resolution falls straight to Spec.Subagent.Image, then helmDefaults.Image.
263	func resolveImage(project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project, level string, helmDefaults ProviderDefaults) string {
264		var levelCfg *tideprojectv1alpha1.LevelConfig
265		if project != nil {
266			switch level {
267			case "milestone":
268				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Milestone
269			case "phase":
270				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Phase
271			case "plan":
272				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Plan
273			case "task":
274				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Task
275			}
276		}
277		switch {
278		case levelCfg != nil && levelCfg.Image != "":
279			return levelCfg.Image
280		case project != nil && project.Spec.Subagent.Image != "":
281			return project.Spec.Subagent.Image
282		default:
283			return helmDefaults.Image
284		}
285	}
286	
287	// checkParentApproval implements the D-02 descent hold — children materialize
288	// but dispatch waits for parental approval (tidal lock pending).
289	//
290	// Returns (true, nil) when the direct parent is parked at AwaitingApproval,
291	// signalling that the child reconciler must hold Job dispatch with a 5s requeue.
292	// Returns (false, nil) when parentName is empty (root level — no parent to check)
293	// or when the parent is not found (client.IgnoreNotFound — NotFound is transient
294	// informer lag; callers continue dispatch and the next reconcile re-checks).
295	// Non-NotFound Get errors propagate to the standard requeue-on-error path.
296	//
297	// The parentKind switch covers "Milestone", "Phase", and "Plan" — the three
298	// parent kinds that can park at AwaitingApproval. Unknown kinds return (false, nil)
299	// (permissive default: unknown parent kind should not block dispatch).
300	//
301	// Design note: held children stay at Status.Phase="" — this helper writes NO
302	// status so tide approve's findAwaiting* cannot target a held child instead of
303	// the parked parent (Pitfall 5 from 12-RESEARCH.md).
304	func checkParentApproval(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, ns, parentName, parentKind string) (bool, error) {
305		if parentName == "" {
306			return false, nil
307		}
308		switch parentKind {
309		case "Milestone":
310			var ms tideprojectv1alpha1.Milestone
311			if err := c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: ns, Name: parentName}, &ms); err != nil {
312				return false, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
313			}
314			return ms.Status.Phase == "AwaitingApproval", nil
315		case "Phase":
316			var ph tideprojectv1alpha1.Phase
317			if err := c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: ns, Name: parentName}, &ph); err != nil {
318				return false, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
319			}
320			return ph.Status.Phase == "AwaitingApproval", nil
321		case "Plan":
322			var plan tideprojectv1alpha1.Plan
323			if err := c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: ns, Name: parentName}, &plan); err != nil {
324				return false, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
325			}
326			return plan.Status.Phase == "AwaitingApproval", nil
327		}
328		return false, nil
329	}

17	package dispatch
18	
19	// ProviderSpec selects the LLM vendor + model + per-vendor tuning knobs the
20	// subagent image should use to satisfy a single dispatch (D-C3). It is carried
21	// on every EnvelopeIn so vendor + model are orthogonal axes from the
22	// orchestrator's point of view: the dispatching reconciler resolves
23	// Project.Spec.subagent.levels.{level}.{vendor,model,params} → Project-level
24	// defaults → Helm-chart defaults and stamps the resolved triple here. The
25	// provider-side Subagent implementation (e.g. internal/subagent/anthropic)
26	// reads Provider.Model to drive its CLI invocation and rejects an envelope
27	// whose Provider.Vendor does not match the image's compiled-in sentinel —
28	// fail-fast defense against config drift between image tag and envelope
29	// content.
30	//
31	// Params is intentionally a string→string map (not a typed struct) so future
32	// per-vendor knobs (Anthropic thinking-budget, OpenAI temperature, Google
33	// safetySettings, etc.) can land without a pkg/dispatch schema bump every
34	// time. Validation of Params keys/values is the provider implementation's
35	// responsibility — pkg/dispatch only guarantees the bytes round-trip.
36	type ProviderSpec struct {
37		// Vendor is the provider sentinel string the subagent image checks at
38		// startup. Canonical values: "anthropic", "openai", "google", "xai",
39		// "opencode". Required (no omitempty) — every dispatch declares a vendor.
40		Vendor string `json:"vendor"`
41	
42		// Model is the per-vendor model identifier passed to the provider CLI or
43		// SDK (e.g. "claude-opus-4-7", "claude-sonnet-4-6", "claude-haiku-4-5",
44		// "o1-mini"). Required (no omitempty).
45		Model string `json:"model"`
46	
47		// Params is the per-vendor tuning passthrough. Keys and values are
48		// vendor-specific; pkg/dispatch performs no semantic validation. Omitted
49		// from JSON when empty so dispatches that pass no tuning don't carry a
50		// "params: {}" placeholder.
51		Params map[string]string `json:"params,omitempty"`
52	}

16	
17	package dispatch
18	
19	import "time"
20	
21	// APIVersionV1Alpha1 is the envelope contract version shipped in TIDE v1alpha1.
22	// Consumers MUST reject envelopes whose apiVersion field does not match this
23	// constant via [ValidateAPIVersionKind].
24	const APIVersionV1Alpha1 = "tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1"
25	
26	// KindTaskEnvelopeIn is the "kind" discriminator for input envelopes written
27	// by the orchestrator and consumed by subagent images.
28	const KindTaskEnvelopeIn = "TaskEnvelopeIn"
29	
30	// KindTaskEnvelopeOut is the "kind" discriminator for output envelopes written
31	// by the in-pod harness and consumed by the controller on Job completion.
32	const KindTaskEnvelopeOut = "TaskEnvelopeOut"
33	
34	// EnvelopeIn is the self-contained input document written by the orchestrator
35	// to /workspace/envelopes/{task-uid}/in.json on the per-Project PVC. The
36	// subagent pod mounts the PVC read-only; everything the agent needs to run is
37	// in this document (D-A4). Out-of-tree subagent image authors decode this type
38	// via [ValidateAPIVersionKind] + json.Unmarshal.
39	type EnvelopeIn struct {
40		// APIVersion is pinned to [APIVersionV1Alpha1]. Harness MUST reject any
41		// other value via [ValidateAPIVersionKind] before consuming any field.
42		APIVersion string `json:"apiVersion"`
43	
44		// Kind is [KindTaskEnvelopeIn]. Harness MUST reject any other value.
45		Kind string `json:"kind"`
46	
47		// TaskUID is the Kubernetes UID of the owning Task CRD object. Carried
48		// through to EnvelopeOut so the controller correlates result envelopes
49		// without scanning the PVC directory tree.
50		TaskUID string `json:"taskUID"`
51	
52		// Role is the planner/executor selector: "planner" or "executor".
53		Role string `json:"role"`
54	
55		// Level is the hierarchy level this task operates at:
56		// "milestone" | "phase" | "plan" | "task".
57		Level string `json:"level"`
58	
59		// Prompt is the full prompt body the subagent should act on.
60		// For executor dispatches this field is empty — the executor reads the
61		// prompt in-pod from PromptPath off its own namespace PVC (defect #10b).
62		// For planner dispatches (role="planner") this field carries the resolved
63		// outcome prompt directly (the outcome prompt is not a PVC artifact).
64		Prompt string `json:"prompt"`
65	
66		// PromptPath is the workspace-relative path of the executor prompt artifact
67		// on the per-Project namespace PVC (e.g.
68		// "envelopes/<plannerUID>/children/task-01.json"). Non-empty only for
69		// executor dispatches (role="executor"). The in-pod anthropic runner reads
70		// the .spec.prompt field from this artifact and renders it as {{.Prompt}}
71		// in the compiled-in prompt template. The Manager MUST NOT read this path
72		// cross-namespace; the read happens in-pod so it uses the executor's own
73		// namespace PVC (defect #10b). Traversal-defended in-pod: absolute paths
74		// and "../" escapes are rejected before the read.
75		PromptPath string `json:"promptPath,omitempty"`
76	
77		// Branch is the per-run git branch (project.Status.Git.BranchName, format
78		// "tide/run-<project>-<unix>") the executor checks out its isolated worktree
79		// onto. Non-empty only for executor dispatches (role="executor"); planner
80		// dispatches short-circuit worktree creation (harness.EnsureWorktree). The
81		// in-pod executor reads this from in.json — the single source of truth for
82		// the worktree branch (09-09: replaced the never-written branch.txt).
83		Branch string `json:"branch,omitempty"`
84	
85		// FilesTouched is the set of repo-relative paths this task is expected to
86		// read or write. Used by the harness for output-path validation (HARN-05)
87		// and by the Plan admission webhook for file-touch consistency checks
88		// (PLAN-02).
89		FilesTouched []string `json:"filesTouched"`
90	
91		// DependsOn is the list of sibling Task names this task declares as
92		// predecessors in the execution DAG. Omitted when empty (D-A4).
93		DependsOn []string `json:"dependsOn,omitempty"`
94	
95		// DeclaredOutputPaths is the exhaustive list of PVC-relative paths the
96		// harness is permitted to write. Any write outside this set is rejected by
97		// the harness (HARN-05).
98		DeclaredOutputPaths []string `json:"declaredOutputPaths"`
99	
100		// Caps carries per-task resource limits enforced by the in-pod harness
101		// (HARN-02). The orchestrator derives these from Project.Spec.budget.
102		Caps Caps `json:"caps"`
103	
104		// ProxyEndpoint is the localhost HTTPS endpoint for the signed-token
105		// credential proxy sidecar (D-C1). Subagent sets ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL to
106		// this value; the sidecar holds the real API key.
107		ProxyEndpoint string `json:"proxyEndpoint"`
108	
109		// SignedToken is the HMAC-SHA256 token minted by the controller at
110		// Job-create time. The credential proxy validates this token before
111		// forwarding API requests (D-C3).
112		SignedToken string `json:"signedToken"`
113	
114		// Provider selects the LLM vendor + model + per-vendor tuning knobs the
115		// subagent image should use to satisfy this dispatch (D-C3). Value type
116		// (not pointer): every dispatch declares a provider. The dispatching
117		// reconciler resolves Project.Spec.subagent.levels.{level}.{vendor,model,
118		// params} → Project-level defaults → Helm-chart defaults and stamps the
119		// resolved triple here. The provider implementation in
120		// internal/subagent/{vendor}/ rejects an envelope whose Provider.Vendor
121		// does not match its compiled-in sentinel — fail-fast defense against
122		// image-tag-vs-envelope drift.
123		//
124		// Provider.Params carries MODEL parameters only (temperature, thinking_budget,
125		// …). It is NOT a general metadata bag — the anthropic runner hard-rejects
126		// any key outside its allow-list. Dispatch metadata the planner needs to
127		// build child-CRD refs lives in [EnvelopeIn.Dispatch], not here.
128		Provider ProviderSpec `json:"provider"`
129	
130		// Dispatch carries orchestrator-side dispatch metadata the planner subagent
131		// reads to build child-CRD references (e.g. parentName → projectRef /
132		// milestoneRef / phaseRef / planRef) without querying the K8s API. It is
133		// distinct from Provider.Params, which is model-parameters-only: overloading
134		// Provider.Params with dispatch metadata tripped the anthropic runner's
135		// strict param allow-list. Pointer + omitempty so executor-level and
136		// real-Claude dispatches that don't consume it don't serialize a
137		// "dispatch: null" placeholder. Real Claude-backed subagent images may
138		// ignore this struct entirely — the real planner authors from the outcome
139		// prompt, not canned parent refs.
140		Dispatch *DispatchMeta `json:"dispatch,omitempty"`
141	
142		// Dev carries test-fixture-only metadata injected by integration tests.
143		// Real Claude-backed subagent images MUST ignore this struct entirely
144		// (RESEARCH.md Pitfall 9 / D-F1). The field is omitted from JSON when nil
145		// so production envelopes are not polluted with "dev: null".
146		Dev *Dev `json:"dev,omitempty"`
147	}
148	
149	// EnvelopeOut is the result document written by the in-pod harness to
150	// /workspace/envelopes/{task-uid}/out.json. The controller reads this file
151	// after the K8s Job terminates to record Task completion status and budget
152	// tally (D-A2).
153	type EnvelopeOut struct {
154		// APIVersion is pinned to [APIVersionV1Alpha1].
155		APIVersion string `json:"apiVersion"`
156	
157		// Kind is [KindTaskEnvelopeOut].
158		Kind string `json:"kind"`
159	
160		// TaskUID mirrors EnvelopeIn.TaskUID so the controller can correlate
161		// result envelopes without scanning directory names.
162		TaskUID string `json:"taskUID"`
163	
164		// ExitCode is the subagent process exit code: 0 = success, non-zero = failure.
165		ExitCode int `json:"exitCode"`
166	
167		// Result is a human-readable one-line summary of the task outcome. Written
168		// by the harness on success or by the stub's canned response on testMode.
169		Result string `json:"result"`
170	
171		// Reason carries a structured failure code when ExitCode != 0, e.g.
172		// "forced-failure", "cap-hit", "output-path-violation", "token-expired".
173		Reason string `json:"reason"`
174	
175		// Usage is the token and cost tally for this task, rolled up by the
176		// controller into Project.Status.budget at Task completion (D-D2).
177		Usage Usage `json:"usage"`
178	
179		// Artifacts is the list of PVC-relative paths the harness confirmed were
180		// written during this task's execution. Used for audit and for the
181		// declared-vs-actual output-path comparison (HARN-05).
182		Artifacts []string `json:"artifacts"`
183	
184		// CompletedAt is the wall-clock time the harness wrote this envelope.
185		CompletedAt time.Time `json:"completedAt"`
186	
187		// ChildCRDs is the slice of typed child-CRD declarations a planner
188		// subagent emits for the orchestrator to materialize server-side (D-A1).
189		// MilestoneReconciler reads Phase entries here; PhaseReconciler reads
190		// Plan entries; PlanReconciler reads Task + Wave entries; TaskReconciler
191		// (executor level) emits no ChildCRDs. Omitted from JSON when empty so
192		// executor-level EnvelopeOut documents stay small. Consumers MUST
193		// validate ChildCRDSpec.Kind against an allowlist before server-side
194		// create — threat T-308 mitigation lives at the consumer site (plan
195		// 03-08), not in this type.
196		ChildCRDs []ChildCRDSpec `json:"childCRDs,omitempty"`
197	
198		// Git carries git-side output a push Job or a harness produces, in
199		// particular the HeadSHA the per-Project run branch was advanced to at
200		// this dispatch's level boundary. Pointer + omitempty so dispatches that
201		// don't touch git (e.g. planner Jobs whose output is consumed only via
202		// child-CRD materialization) don't serialize a "git: null" placeholder.
203		Git *GitOutput `json:"git,omitempty"`
204	
205		// ChildCount is the number of child CRDs the planner authored. When
206		// reading the tiny termination-message status (PodStatusEnvelopeReader),
207		// this field is populated from the TerminationStub.ChildCount JSON key so
208		// planner controllers can perform race-free succession gating without also
209		// carrying the full ChildCRDs payloads (plan 09-08 Defect B). Zero for
210		// executor-level dispatches and genuine leaf planners. When reading a full
211		// out.json via FilesystemEnvelopeReader the field falls back to
212		// len(ChildCRDs) in the planner controllers (non-Option-C path).
213		ChildCount int `json:"childCount,omitempty"`
214	}
215	
216	// GitOutput carries git-side output fields a dispatch produces, populated by
217	// the push Job at level-boundary push success and by executor harnesses
218	// committing per-Task worktree changes (D-B4 / D-B6). HeadSHA is the SHA the
219	// per-run branch was advanced to; ProjectReconciler patches
220	// Project.Status.git.lastPushedSHA from this value to enable the next push's
221	// --force-with-lease check (Pitfall 13 mitigation).
222	type GitOutput struct {
223		// HeadSHA is the 40-character hex SHA the per-run branch points to after
224		// this dispatch's level-boundary push completed. Required when Git is
225		// non-nil.
226		HeadSHA string `json:"headSHA"`
227	}
228	
229	// Caps carries per-task resource limits (HARN-02). The orchestrator derives
230	// these from Project.Spec.budget and injects them into every EnvelopeIn so
231	// the in-pod harness can enforce them without querying the K8s API.
232	type Caps struct {
233		// WallClockSeconds is the maximum wall-clock seconds the subagent is
234		// permitted to run before the harness sends SIGTERM.
235		WallClockSeconds int `json:"wallClockSeconds"`
236	
237		// Iterations is the maximum number of agent loop iterations before the
238		// harness sends SIGTERM.
239		Iterations int `json:"iterations"`
240	
241		// InputTokens is the maximum cumulative input tokens across all LLM calls.
242		InputTokens int64 `json:"inputTokens"`
243	
244		// OutputTokens is the maximum cumulative output tokens across all LLM
245		// calls in this task.
246		OutputTokens int64 `json:"outputTokens"`
247	}
248	
249	// Usage carries the token and cost tally reported by the harness in
250	// EnvelopeOut. Rolled up by the controller into Project.Status.budget at Task
251	// completion (D-D2).
252	type Usage struct {
253		// InputTokens is the total input tokens consumed across all LLM calls
254		// during this task's execution.
255		InputTokens int64 `json:"inputTokens"`
256	
257		// OutputTokens is the total output tokens produced across all LLM calls
258		// during this task's execution.
259		OutputTokens int64 `json:"outputTokens"`
260	
261		// EstimatedCostCents is the estimated US-cent cost for this task, rounded
262		// up to the nearest cent. Used for Project.Status.budget.costSpentCents
263		// rollup.
264		EstimatedCostCents int64 `json:"estimatedCostCents"`
265	
266		// Iterations is the actual number of agent loop iterations completed.
267		Iterations int `json:"iterations"`
268	
269		// CacheReadTokens is the count of input tokens served from the provider's
270		// prompt cache (D-C5). The Anthropic stream-json
271		// usage.cache_read_input_tokens field maps directly here. Surfacing this
272		// separately lets Project.Status.budget (Phase 2 D-D2) show the cached
273		// vs uncached input mix instead of hiding cache hits inside InputTokens.
274		CacheReadTokens int64 `json:"cacheReadTokens"`
275	
276		// CacheCreationTokens is the count of input tokens spent populating the
277		// provider's prompt cache during this dispatch (D-C5). The Anthropic
278		// stream-json usage.cache_creation_input_tokens field maps directly here.
279		// Cache creation is billed at a higher rate than cache reads; separating
280		// the two prevents budget rollups from understating real spend on
281		// cache-warmup turns.
282		CacheCreationTokens int64 `json:"cacheCreationTokens"`
283	}
284	
285	// DispatchMeta carries orchestrator-side dispatch metadata the planner subagent
286	// reads to build child-CRD references without querying the K8s API. It is kept
287	// separate from [ProviderSpec.Params] (model-parameters-only) so the anthropic
288	// runner's strict param allow-list is not tripped by dispatch metadata. Real
289	// Claude-backed subagent images may ignore this struct — the real planner
290	// authors child CRDs from the outcome prompt, not canned parent refs.
291	type DispatchMeta struct {
292		// ParentName is the up-stack CRD's metadata.name. The stub planner stamps
293		// it into the child CRD's parent-spec ref (projectRef / milestoneRef /
294		// phaseRef / planRef) so the controller can wire ownership without the
295		// subagent holding a K8s client (T-07-03-03: parentName is metadata, not a
296		// secret).
297		ParentName string `json:"parentName,omitempty"`
298	}
299	
300	// Dev carries test-fixture-only metadata for the stub-subagent image. Real
301	// Claude-backed subagent images MUST ignore this struct entirely (D-F1 /
302	// RESEARCH.md Pitfall 9). It is intentionally kept in a sub-struct so the
303	// compiler makes omission explicit (pointer + omitempty on EnvelopeIn.Dev) and
304	// grep for "Dev" in production code is unambiguous.
305	type Dev struct {
306		// TestMode is the stub behavior selector. Valid values:
307		//   "success"              — writes canned result.json + declared artifacts; exit 0.
308		//   "fail-exit-1"          — writes structured failure result; exit 1.
309		//   "hang"                 — sleeps past caps.wallClockSeconds (HARN-02 test).
310		//   "exceed-output-paths"  — writes outside declaredOutputPaths (HARN-05 test).
311		// An empty string is treated as "success" by the stub.
312		TestMode string `json:"testMode,omitempty"`
313	}
314	
315	// TerminationStub is the tiny cross-namespace status carrier written to the
316	// dispatch Job's 4 KB termination message (Pod.status.containerStatuses[].
317	// state.terminated.message). It carries ONLY the fields the Manager needs to
318	// record Task completion without reading the PVC: ExitCode, Reason, Usage,
319	// the HeadSHA the run-branch was advanced to (if any), and ChildCount — the
320	// count of child CRDs the planner authored. ChildCRDs payloads and the verbose
321	// Result are intentionally excluded — they stay on the namespace-local PVC in
322	// out.json, which the in-namespace reporter Job reads (plan 09-05).
323	//
324	// ChildCount enables race-free succession gating (plan 09-08 Defect B): the
325	// controller compares observed owned children against this expected count and
326	// requeues until observed >= expected before declaring the level Succeeded.
327	//
328	// By construction this type MUST stay < 4 KB when marshalled; the test
329	// TestNewTerminationStub_StaysSmall enforces this invariant.
330	type TerminationStub struct {
331		// ExitCode mirrors EnvelopeOut.ExitCode: 0 = success, non-zero = failure.
332		ExitCode int `json:"exitCode"`
333	
334		// Reason mirrors EnvelopeOut.Reason: structured failure code (e.g.
335		// "forced-failure", "cap-hit") when ExitCode != 0.
336		Reason string `json:"reason"`
337	
338		// Usage mirrors EnvelopeOut.Usage: the token and cost tally for this
339		// dispatch, rolled up by the Manager into Project.Status.budget.
340		Usage Usage `json:"usage"`
341	
342		// HeadSHA is the git HeadSHA from EnvelopeOut.Git.HeadSHA, flattened here
343		// to avoid a nested pointer. Empty when the dispatch did not produce a git
344		// push (executor-level, non-push Jobs). The Manager copies this into
345		// Project.Status.git.lastPushedSHA for the --force-with-lease fence.
346		HeadSHA string `json:"headSHA,omitempty"`
347	
348		// ChildCount is the number of child CRDs the planner authored
349		// (len(EnvelopeOut.ChildCRDs)). Used by the four planner-level controllers
350		// as the "expected" child count for race-free succession gating: a level
351		// cannot Succeed until observed owned children >= ChildCount (plan 09-08).
352		// Zero for executor-level dispatches and genuine leaf planners.
353		ChildCount int `json:"childCount"`
354	}
355	
356	// NewTerminationStub builds a TerminationStub from a full EnvelopeOut by
357	// copying the tiny-status subset: ExitCode, Reason, Usage, Git.HeadSHA, and
358	// ChildCount (= len(out.ChildCRDs)). ChildCRD payloads, Result, and Artifacts
359	// are deliberately excluded. A nil out.Git is safe and produces an empty
360	// HeadSHA. A nil or empty ChildCRDs slice produces ChildCount == 0.
361	func NewTerminationStub(out EnvelopeOut) TerminationStub {
362		headSHA := ""
363		if out.Git != nil {
364			headSHA = out.Git.HeadSHA
365		}
366		return TerminationStub{
367			ExitCode:   out.ExitCode,
368			Reason:     out.Reason,
369			Usage:      out.Usage,
370			HeadSHA:    headSHA,
371			ChildCount: len(out.ChildCRDs),
372		}
373	}
374	
375	// ValidateAPIVersionKind checks that apiVersion equals [APIVersionV1Alpha1]
376	// and that kind equals expectedKind. It is the first call an envelope consumer
377	// must make before accessing any other field (D-A3).
378	//
379	// Returns [*UnknownAPIVersionError] if apiVersion is unrecognized.
380	// Returns [*UnknownKindError] if kind is unrecognized.
381	// Returns nil on success.
382	func ValidateAPIVersionKind(apiVersion, kind, expectedKind string) error {
383		if apiVersion != APIVersionV1Alpha1 {
384			return &UnknownAPIVersionError{APIVersion: apiVersion}
385		}
386		if kind != expectedKind {
387			return &UnknownKindError{Kind: kind}
388		}
389		return nil
390	}

16	
17	package anthropic
18	
19	import (
20		"encoding/json"
21		"fmt"
22		"io"
23	
24		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/subagent/common"
25		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
26	)
27	
28	// streamEvent is the verified Claude Code stream-json line shape (RESEARCH
29	// Pattern 5 lines 502-523). Only the fields Phase 3 actually consumes are
30	// declared; everything else (subtype, session_id, durations, model_usage)
31	// rides through to events.jsonl untouched for Phase 4 OpenInference parsing.
32	//
33	// JSON tags use snake_case to match the CLI's top-level shape. The per-model
34	// nested model_usage uses camelCase per the CLI's TypeScript pass-through —
35	// Phase 3 ignores that nested map because Phase 2's Usage struct does not
36	// expose per-model breakdown yet.
37	type streamEvent struct {
38		Type   string       `json:"type"`
39		Result string       `json:"result,omitempty"`
40		Usage  *streamUsage `json:"usage,omitempty"`
41	}
42	
43	// streamUsage mirrors the top-level `usage` block in a stream-json result
44	// event. Field names use snake_case to match what the CLI emits; the parser
45	// maps them to camelCase pkg/dispatch.Usage fields per D-C5 (CacheReadTokens,
46	// CacheCreationTokens drop the "Input" infix because Phase 2's
47	// pkg/dispatch.Usage already separates input/output via dedicated fields).
48	type streamUsage struct {
49		InputTokens              int64 `json:"input_tokens"`
50		OutputTokens             int64 `json:"output_tokens"`
51		CacheReadInputTokens     int64 `json:"cache_read_input_tokens"`
52		CacheCreationInputTokens int64 `json:"cache_creation_input_tokens"`
53	}
54	
55	// ParseStream reads JSONL stream-json events from r, tees the raw bytes to
56	// rawSink (the per-Task events.jsonl audit log for Phase 4 OpenInference),
57	// and returns the final assistant text + token tally extracted from the
58	// terminal result event.
59	//
60	// Per D-C5: maps snake_case stream-json keys → camelCase pkg/dispatch.Usage
61	// fields:
62	//
63	//	input_tokens                → Usage.InputTokens
64	//	output_tokens               → Usage.OutputTokens
65	//	cache_read_input_tokens     → Usage.CacheReadTokens
66	//	cache_creation_input_tokens → Usage.CacheCreationTokens
67	//
68	// Defensive parsing: non-JSON lines (rare per RESEARCH Pattern 5) are written
69	// to rawSink and silently skipped — they do not cause ParseStream to return
70	// an error. The rationale (RESEARCH Pattern 5 line 555): "tolerate non-JSON
71	// lines (rare, but defensive)" — the audit log records everything; the
72	// orchestrator gets the structured tally.
73	//
74	// The 16MB per-line budget is enforced by [common.ReadLines]; lines longer
75	// than that return an error.
76	func ParseStream(r io.Reader, rawSink io.Writer) (pkgdispatch.Usage, string, error) {
77		var (
78			usage      pkgdispatch.Usage
79			resultText string
80		)
81	
82		err := common.ReadLines(r, func(line []byte) error {
83			// Tee raw bytes (with newline) to events.jsonl. Phase 4 OpenInference
84			// parsing reads this file untouched — Phase 3 only consumes the
85			// terminal result event.
86			if _, werr := rawSink.Write(append(line, '\n')); werr != nil {
87				return fmt.Errorf("anthropic: write events.jsonl: %w", werr)
88			}
89	
90			var ev streamEvent
91			if jerr := json.Unmarshal(line, &ev); jerr != nil {
92				// Tolerate non-JSON lines — they have already been teed to
93				// rawSink for Phase 4 forensic analysis.
94				return nil
95			}
96	
97			if ev.Type == "result" {
98				resultText = ev.Result
99				if ev.Usage != nil {
100					usage.InputTokens = ev.Usage.InputTokens
101					usage.OutputTokens = ev.Usage.OutputTokens
102					// D-C5 snake_case → camelCase mapping. Drop the "Input"
103					// infix on cache fields: pkg/dispatch.Usage separates input
104					// vs output via dedicated fields, so qualifying the cache
105					// counters with "Input" would be redundant.
106					usage.CacheReadTokens = ev.Usage.CacheReadInputTokens
107					usage.CacheCreationTokens = ev.Usage.CacheCreationInputTokens
108				}
109			}
110			return nil
111		})
112		if err != nil {
113			return usage, resultText, err
114		}
115		return usage, resultText, nil
116	}
117

# TIDE controller chart values (Phase 1 surface — D-E1).
#
# This file is the canonical, hand-maintained values.yaml for charts/tide/. The
# `make helm-controller` target generates charts/tide/ via helmify, then copies
# THIS file over the helmify-emitted charts/tide/values.yaml. Helmify cannot
# infer (a) ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* image registry, (b) the Phase 1 tunables
# (plannerConcurrency, executorConcurrency, maxConcurrentReconciles), or (c)
# leaderElection block — those are project-level decisions that live here,
# version-controlled separately from helmify's regenerated output.
#
# Phase 5 will extend this with dashboard.enabled, prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled,
# and additional OSS-distribution flags (D-E2). The structure below — helmify-emitted
# controllerManager.* keys plus hand-augmented top-level Phase 1 tunables — is the
# Phase 5-compatible foundation.

# Image refs (D-A2: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* registry).
controllerManager:
  manager:
    args:
    - --metrics-bind-address=:8443
    - --config=/etc/tide/config.yaml
    - --watch-namespace=$(WATCH_NAMESPACE)
    - --leader-elect
    - --webhook-cert-path=/tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs
    containerSecurityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - ALL
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
    env:
      # watchNamespace = "" means watch ALL namespaces (cluster-scoped install posture,
      # AUTH-02). Set this to a specific namespace for per-namespace install — the
      # cmd/manager/main.go --watch-namespace flag wires it through to the Manager's
      # cache.ByObject namespace filter.
      watchNamespace: ""
    image:
      repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-controller
      tag: ""
      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    podAnnotations: {}
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: 500m
        memory: 128Mi
      requests:
        cpu: 10m
        memory: 64Mi
  podSecurityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccount:
    annotations: {}
kubernetesClusterDomain: cluster.local
metricsService:
  ports:
  - name: https
    port: 8443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 8443
  type: ClusterIP
webhookService:
  ports:
  - port: 443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 9443
  type: ClusterIP

# Phase 1 runtime tunables (D-E1) — rendered into the tide-config ConfigMap which is
# mounted at /etc/tide/config.yaml and parsed by internal/config.Load (CTRL-04 / Plan 04).
#
# Two distinct parallelism budgets per Pitfall 6: planning fans out wide (Milestone/
# Phase/Plan reconciles), execution fans out narrow (Wave/Task reconciles). The
# crosspool analyzer (cmd/tide-lint) prevents the two budgets from being unified at
# compile time; this values surface keeps them separately tunable at deploy time.
plannerConcurrency: 16
executorConcurrency: 4

# Per-Kind controller-runtime MaxConcurrentReconciles. Values match the Plan 04
# defaults — see internal/config/defaults.go. Project/Milestone are singletons-per-
# parent in the common case (1 is enough); Wave/Task fan out wider.
maxConcurrentReconciles:
  project: 1
  milestone: 1
  phase: 2
  plan: 4
  wave: 8
  task: 16

# CTRL-03 leader election. Enabled by default; the controller's
# --leader-elect arg is appended in controllerManager.manager.args above.
#
# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-D1 chaos-resume tuning): leaseDurationSeconds,
# renewDeadlineSeconds, and retryPeriodSeconds flow through to
# controller-runtime via TIDE_LEADER_{LEASE,RENEW,RETRY}_SECONDS env vars on
# the manager Deployment. Defaults give a single-pod restart ≤25s failover
# ceiling. Invariant: lease > renew > retry (controller-runtime validates).
leaderElection:

17	package v1alpha1
18	
19	import (
20		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
21	)
22	
23	// SecretRefs declares the K8s Secret names that carry credentials.
24	// Per AUTH-01, populated in Phase 3; Phase 1 ships the field shape only.
25	type SecretRefs struct {
26		// AnthropicAPIKey is the Secret name carrying the LLM API key.
27		// +optional
28		AnthropicAPIKey string `json:"anthropicAPIKey,omitempty"`
29		// GitCredentials is the Secret name carrying git push credentials (PAT or SSH key).
30		// +optional
31		GitCredentials string `json:"gitCredentials,omitempty"`
32	}
33	
34	// ModelSelection picks per-level model identifiers.
35	// Phase 1 ships the field shape; Phase 2+ consumes.
36	type ModelSelection struct {
37		// +optional
38		Milestone string `json:"milestone,omitempty"`
39		// +optional
40		Phase string `json:"phase,omitempty"`
41		// +optional
42		Plan string `json:"plan,omitempty"`
43		// +optional
44		Task string `json:"task,omitempty"`
45	}
46	
47	// GatePolicy is one of "auto" | "approve" | "pause" — per-level human gate.
48	// Phase 1 ships the field shape; Phase 4 consumes.
49	// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=auto;approve;pause
50	type GatePolicy string
51	
52	// Gates declares per-level gate policy. Phase 1 ships field; Phase 4 wires.
53	type Gates struct {
54		// +optional
55		Milestone GatePolicy `json:"milestone,omitempty"`
56		// +optional
57		Phase GatePolicy `json:"phase,omitempty"`
58		// +optional
59		Plan GatePolicy `json:"plan,omitempty"`
60		// +optional
61		Task GatePolicy `json:"task,omitempty"`
62		// +optional
63		PauseBetweenWaves bool `json:"pauseBetweenWaves,omitempty"`
64	}
65	
66	// RouteSpec is a (method, path-prefix) tuple used to extend the credproxy
67	// upstream allowlist for a Project (Phase 04.1 P4.2). The credproxy sidecar
68	// always enforces the hardcoded baseline (POST /v1/messages and
69	// POST /v1/messages/count_tokens); RouteSpec entries are additive extensions,
70	// never replacements.
71	type RouteSpec struct {
72		// Method is the HTTP method. Currently only GET and POST are accepted.
73		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=GET;POST
74		Method string `json:"method"`
75	
76		// PathPrefix is the URL path prefix. Must start with "/v1/" — paths
77		// outside the Anthropic API surface are rejected at admission.
78		// +kubebuilder:validation:Pattern=`^/v1/[a-zA-Z0-9_/-]+$`
79		PathPrefix string `json:"pathPrefix"`
80	}

85		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=anthropic
86		Name string `json:"name"`
87	
88		// RequestsPerMinute optionally caps API requests per minute for this provider.
89		// +optional
90		RequestsPerMinute *int32 `json:"requestsPerMinute,omitempty"`
91	
92		// TokensPerMinute optionally caps token throughput per minute for this provider.
93		// +optional
94		TokensPerMinute *int32 `json:"tokensPerMinute,omitempty"`
95	
96		// AllowedRoutes optionally extends the hardcoded credproxy allowlist
97		// (POST /v1/messages + POST /v1/messages/count_tokens) with additional
98		// (method, path-prefix) tuples. Use this to grant the subagent access
99		// to newly-released LLM endpoints (Files API, Search Tools, prompt
100		// caching) without rebuilding the credproxy image.
101		// Hardcoded safe defaults ALWAYS apply — operator additions are
102		// additive, never restrictive. Admin/billing paths are rejected at
103		// admission (defense in depth).
104		// +optional

108	// BudgetConfig declares cost/token caps for the Project (Phase 2+).

111		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=0

114		// RollingWindowCapCents caps spending over the rolling window defined by

118		// +optional

121		// RollingWindowDuration is the window length over which

123		// to prevent inadvertent denial-of-dispatch via tiny windows.
124		// Validation: enforced semantically in ProjectReconciler (metav1.Duration
125		// is a struct; controller-gen Pattern markers require a string type).

226	// GitStatus records the per-Project git push state (Phase 3 D-B6).

228	// One branch per Project lifetime: BranchName is fixed at Project creation
229	// as "tide/run-<project-name>-<unix-epoch>" (Unix epoch keeps refnames
230	// colon-free per RFC 3987 refname rules). LastPushedSHA + LeaseFailureCount
231	// drive the --force-with-lease push contract: each push uses lastPushedSHA
232	// as the lease, increments leaseFailureCount on rejection, and resets to 0
233	// on success.

235		// BranchName is the lifetime branch fixed at Project creation.

247		// count exceeds the configured retry budget.

272	// boundary push (debug defect #13b). The boundary push lands the already-

276	// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: this is a per-project retry tally object, NOT an
277	// aggregate schedule. Attempts is re-derived from .status on every reconcile so
278	// the bounded retry survives a controller restart (no in-memory counter).
279	type BoundaryPushStatus struct {
280		// Attempts is the number of boundary-push Jobs dispatched so far for this

382		PhasePushLeaseFailed = "PushLeaseFailed"

384		// finding — envelope.reason=leak-detected). Distinct from PhasePushLeaseFailed

390		// PhaseComplete is the terminal success phase — set when all Milestones
391		// reach Succeeded and the final push Job lands (Phase 3 D-B2 #4).
392		PhaseComplete = "Complete"

#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:<tag>
# Bare refs (no ":<tag>" or "@sha256:" suffix) have ":<appVersion>" appended by the
# chart template. Tag- or digest-qualified values pass through verbatim.
# Digest pinning is recommended for production reproducibility:
#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent@sha256:<digest>
#
# Per-level model defaults (D-C4) match the phase-brief lock-in:
#   milestone → claude-opus-4-7    (heavy planning, lowest fan-out)
#   phase     → claude-sonnet-4-6
#   plan      → claude-sonnet-4-6
#   task      → claude-haiku-4-5   (highest fan-out, cost-bounded)
#
# Per-level models flow through the manager Deployment env block as
# TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_{MILESTONE,PHASE,PLAN,TASK}.
subagent:
  defaults:
    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
    model: claude-sonnet-4-6
  levels:
    milestone:
      model: claude-opus-4-7
    phase:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    plan:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    task:
      model: claude-haiku-4-5

# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B3): optional cluster-wide gitleaks override.
# Empty (default) → push Job uses the compiled-in gitleaks v8 ruleset.
# Per-Project overrides are still supported via Project.Spec.git.leaksConfigRef
# (D-B3 — ConfigMap mounted into push Job at /etc/tide/gitleaks-config.toml).
# This top-level key is the cluster-wide fallback (v1.0 deferred; reserved for v1.x).
gitleaks:
  configMapName: ""

# HMAC signing-secret name (D-C3 / Blocker #1 fix).
# The signing-secret.yaml Helm template creates a Secret with this name on first install
# using Helm's `lookup` + `if not` guard (idempotent). resource-policy: keep annotation
# preserves the Secret across helm upgrade/uninstall so in-flight tokens stay valid
# (T-02-12-02 / T-02-12-03 mitigations).
# The Secret's data key is TIDE_SIGNING_KEY (env-friendly) so envFrom: [{secretRef: ...}]
# auto-populates the TIDE_SIGNING_KEY env var on the Manager Deployment and credproxy sidecar.
signingKey:
  secretName: tide-signing-key

# Budget defaults (D-D2 / FAIL-04). Helm-driven hard caps applied to all Projects that
# do not specify per-Project caps in Project.Spec.Budget.
budget:
  # D-05: flat per-dispatch reservation estimate in cents pre-charged against the budget
  # cap at Job creation; bounds in-flight overshoot to estimate error instead of wave width;
  # 0 disables pre-charge (cap still enforced at dispatch via the BudgetBlocked condition).
  # Passed via --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents.
  reservePerDispatchCents: 100
  defaults:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000      # $100.00 default absolute cap
    rollingWindowCapCents: 5000  # $50.00 / 15-min rolling window default

# Phase 4 additions (Plan 04-14 — D-X3 / D-D2 / D-O6 / D-O3).
#
# All three blocks are ADDITIVE. The Phase 1 surface above is unchanged
# (CLAUDE.md "chart is FIXED contract — binary catches up to chart, never
# reverse" anti-pattern). Phase 4 chart additions land here without touching
# any pre-Phase-4 keys.

# Read-only dashboard backend (Phase 4 D-D2). Ships as a separate
# Deployment + Service + ServiceAccount + ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding
# under templates/dashboard-{deployment,service,rbac}.yaml. The dashboard
# ClusterRole is enforced read-only at chart-render time via
# `make helm-rbac-assert` (T-04-D2 mitigation).
#
# Default ON because the dashboard is one of the four Phase 4 deliverables;
# operators who want the controller-only install set
# `--set dashboard.enabled=false`.
dashboard:
  enabled: true
  image:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-dashboard
    tag: ""             # default to .Chart.AppVersion
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  replicas: 1
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 50m
      memory: 64Mi
    limits:
      cpu: 200m
      memory: 256Mi
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
    port: 80
    targetPort: 8080

tool_use_result.stdout:

# (T-02-12-02 / T-02-12-03 mitigations).
# The Secret's data key is TIDE_SIGNING_KEY (env-friendly) so envFrom: [{secretRef: ...}]
# auto-populates the TIDE_SIGNING_KEY env var on the Manager Deployment and credproxy sidecar.
signingKey:
  secretName: tide-signing-key

# Budget defaults (D-D2 / FAIL-04). Helm-driven hard caps applied to all Projects that
# do not specify per-Project caps in Project.Spec.Budget.
budget:
  # D-05: flat per-dispatch reservation estimate in cents pre-charged against the budget
  # cap at Job creation; bounds in-flight overshoot to estimate error instead of wave width;
  # 0 disables pre-charge (cap still enforced at dispatch via the BudgetBlocked condition).
  # Passed via --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents.
  reservePerDispatchCents: 100
  defaults:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000      # $100.00 default absolute cap
    rollingWindowCapCents: 5000  # $50.00 / 15-min rolling window default

# Phase 4 additions (Plan 04-14 — D-X3 / D-D2 / D-O6 / D-O3).
#
# All three blocks are ADDITIVE. The Phase 1 surface above is unchanged
# (CLAUDE.md "chart is FIXED contract — binary catches up to chart, never
# reverse" anti-pattern). Phase 4 chart additions land here without touching
# any pre-Phase-4 keys.

# Read-only dashboard backend (Phase 4 D-D2). Ships as a separate
# Deployment + Service + ServiceAccount + ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding
# under templates/dashboard-{deployment,service,rbac}.yaml. The dashboard
# ClusterRole is enforced read-only at chart-render time via
# `make helm-rbac-assert` (T-04-D2 mitigation).
#
# Default ON because the dashboard is one of the four Phase 4 deliverables;
# operators who want the controller-only install set
# `--set dashboard.enabled=false`.
dashboard:
  enabled: true
  image:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-dashboard
    tag: ""             # default to .Chart.AppVersion
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  replicas: 1
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 50m
      memory: 64Mi
    limits:
      cpu: 200m
      memory: 256Mi
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
    port: 80
    targetPort: 8080

# Prometheus ServiceMonitor (Phase 4 D-O6).
#

# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
# Build the claude-subagent runtime image.
# Invoked from REPO ROOT:
#   docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:test \
#                -f images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile .
#
# Stage 1: compile a static Linux Go binary.
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.26-alpine@sha256:a6a091eac01ceac4b97496fe2957a49b6cdd83365337d5f46f6f73710424e805 AS builder
ARG TARGETOS
ARG TARGETARCH

WORKDIR /workspace

# Cache the dependency layer before copying source.
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod go mod download

# Copy only what the binary needs (see .dockerignore for exclusions).
COPY pkg/dispatch/ pkg/dispatch/
COPY internal/harness/ internal/harness/
COPY internal/subagent/ internal/subagent/
COPY pkg/git/ pkg/git/
COPY cmd/claude-subagent/ cmd/claude-subagent/

# Static binary: no CGO, stripped symbols for a smaller image.
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=${TARGETOS:-linux} GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
      -ldflags="-s -w" \
      -o /out/claude-subagent \
      ./cmd/claude-subagent

# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
#
# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.
FROM node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732

RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    # The per-Project repo (/workspace/repo.git) is cloned by the tide-push Job
    # (uid 65532) but the executor runs as uid 1000 — git 2.35+ refuses a repo
    # it does not own ("detected dubious ownership"). The pod is the trust
    # boundary (hermetic, single-purpose), so mark all repos safe at the system
    # level. This covers our `git worktree add` shell-out AND any git the claude
    # CLI itself runs inside the worktree (whose .git points back to repo.git).
    && git config --system --add safe.directory '*'

RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.142

COPY --from=builder /out/claude-subagent /usr/local/bin/claude-subagent

# Run as non-root UID 1000 — matches D-G3 subagent harness UID requirement.
USER 1000

ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/claude-subagent"]

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Working Rules

Project-agnostic operating principles. Apply these before anything else, including before reading the rest of this file.

### 1. Observe First

Every wasted iteration in Phase 02.2 traced back to acting on assumptions instead of observations. The cascade-5 debug session hypothesized a "Wave Ready check" that didn't exist; the cascade-8 debug session hypothesized "ResourceVersion-conflict exponential-backoff suppression" when the actual cause was a never-assigned `Dispatcher` field. Both were avoidable.

1. When a runtime gate is BLOCKED → **read the manager log (`kubectl logs` or `/tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log`) and the VERIFICATION.md frontmatter BEFORE hypothesizing**
2. When a controller misbehaves → **`kubectl logs` + `kubectl describe <crd>` + `kubectl get <crd> -o yaml` BEFORE reading reconciler source**
3. When a debug hypothesis cites a specific code path → **grep the codebase to confirm it exists in the cited form** (`grep -nE "Wave.*Ready|Ready.*Wave" internal/controller/` would have refuted cascade-5's framing in seconds)
4. When a code change has no visible effect → **verify the binary rebuilt (image digest changed) and the Pod rotated (`kubectl get pods -n tide-system` shows new age)**, don't keep tweaking
5. When suggesting a Makefile target → **`grep -nE '^target-name:' Makefile`** to confirm it exists before recommending the command
6. When an agent could answer a question → **but `kubectl get`, `git log --grep`, or a single grep answers it faster, use the CLI**

Subagents are for understanding architecture and authoring artifacts. Runtime artifacts (manager logs, CRD status, git history) are for debugging runtime failures. Do not confuse the two.

### 2. Execute, Don't Ask

When the next step is obvious and non-destructive AND inside an active GSD workflow: do it. Don't present the command and ask for confirmation. This includes running `make test-int`, rerunning failing tests, creating planning artifacts, applying fixes specified in an approved `PLAN.md`, and committing per the executor's task protocol.

**Exceptions — always confirm or refuse:**

- **Production code edits outside an active GSD plan.** The `GSD Workflow Enforcement` section below is binding. Even if the fix is obvious, route through `/gsd:quick` or `/gsd:debug` so artifacts stay in sync.
- **Edits to `charts/tide/values.yaml`** — chart is FIXED contract per Phase 02.2's anti-pattern. Binary catches up to chart, never reverse.
- **`git push --force`** to any branch, especially `main`.
- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.
- **`kind delete cluster`** outside an explicit clean-rerun sequence (it destroys all test state).
- **`rm -rf`** on `.git/`, `.planning/`, `.claude/`, or any directory containing uncommitted user work.
- **`gsd-sdk query state-set ...`** that downgrades phase status or rewrites tracked progress fields.

### 3. Verify Before Claiming

Never say "this should fix it," "the fix is complete," or "cascade-N is closed." Run the verification. Read the gate_decision. Grep for the artifact. Then state what you observed.

Concrete verification protocols for Phase 02.2-style runtime gates:

- **Plan landed:** `git log --oneline -1 -- <files_modified>` shows the expected commit on `main`
- **VERIFICATION.md exists:** `ls .planning/phases/<dir>/<plan>-VERIFICATION.md` returns a file
- **Gate APPROVED:** `grep -cE '^gate_decision: APPROVED$' <VERIFICATION>` returns exactly `1`
- **Test ran:** `tail -30 /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` shows Ginkgo summary line `Ran X Specs in Ys`
- **Dispatch is live (Phase 02.2-10 evidence shape):** `grep -E '"creating job"|"dispatch"' /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` returns ≥ 1 line, AND `kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces` shows Jobs in the test namespaces
- **Cascade closed:** the specific manager-log error that defined the cascade (e.g. `"no project found in namespace X"`) returns 0 in the new run's log
- **`make test-int` exit ≠ "Ginkgo green."** The `test/integration/kind` package bundles plain go-tests (e.g. the helm-template contract tests like `TestHelmDeploymentTemplateRendersManagerPodAnnotations`) alongside the Ginkgo specs. One RED go-test fails the package and trips `make test-int` non-zero **even when both layers print `Ran X of Y … SUCCESS!`**. Always read the echoed `MAKE_EXIT` AND `grep -nE '^--- FAIL|^FAIL\s'` the log — not just the Ginkgo summary. (Phase 7 nearly tagged v1.0 on "14/14 green" while `make test-int` was red on a dropped chart-template block.)
- **A subagent's "pre-existing / unrelated" dismissal of a failing test is a claim, not verification.** Before relaying it as safe, git-archaeology the regressing commit (`git log -S '<token>' -- <file>`) and confirm it does not fail the gate. (Two Phase-7 agents waved off the dropped `podAnnotations` render block as pre-existing; it was a real ship-blocker — the verifier caught it and a `make test-int` re-run proved it.)

## Operating Notes (Phase 02.2 lessons)

Rules learned from 10 BLOCKED iterations across Phase 02.2. Trust them.

- **Don't predict "chain terminator."** Plans 02.2-08/09/10 each predicted the end; all surfaced new cascades. Frame work as iterations; don't anchor on terminations.
- **Pre-empt worktree cwd-drift before merging.** Executor agents occasionally write artifacts to main's working tree instead of the worktree's. Pre-merge check:
  ```
  UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | grep '^??' | grep "<plan>-" || true)
  ```
  If non-empty, diff against the worktree branch's commits; remove duplicates before `git merge`. (Plan 02.2-09 merge failed without this; Plan 02.2-10 succeeded with it.)

# examples/projects/medium/project.yaml — Phase 5 D-B2 medium sample (~$5 real Claude).
#
# This sample lets operators run TIDE against real Claude (Haiku 4.5, cost-disciplined
# per D-B1) with a hard $5 cap — no external public repo dependency (D-B3). The
# targetRepo is an in-cluster http:// git remote served by the tide-git-http-server
# Deployment (git http-backend CGI + nginx), backed by the demo-remote-pvc populated
# by the demo-remote-init Job. All git transport is pure-Go (go-git HTTP) — no git
# binary in core images required.
#
# APPLY ORDER (do NOT apply this file alone — see README.md):
#   1. namespace.yaml
#   2. demo-remote-pvc.yaml
#   3. per-namespace-resources.yaml      (tide-projects PVC + tide-subagent SA)
#   4. Mirror tide-signing-key Secret from tide-system (see per-namespace-resources.yaml)
#   5. demo-remote-init-job.yaml         (init Job populates the bare repo on demo-remote-pvc)
#      kubectl wait --for=condition=Complete job/demo-remote-init \
#        -n tide-sample-medium --timeout=2m

    absoluteCapCents: 500
    rollingWindowCapCents: 500

    rollingWindowDuration: 24h

  subagent:

# examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml
#
# Run 2 of 3 in the TIDE-on-TIDE dogfood sequence.
# Apply ONLY after 01-analytics-project reaches `status.phase: Complete`.
# Do NOT apply 03-project-editor-project.yaml until this Project reaches
# `status.phase: Complete` — the Project CRD has no ordering mechanism;
# sequencing is operator discipline.
#
# Purpose: add a second Subagent implementation (Codex / OpenAI) to TIDE and
# demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — actually running mixed-provider
# waves, not just proving the interface compiles.
#
# After this run completes: the run's token spend, dispatch behavior, and
# failure characteristics are visible on the analytics dashboard built by run 1.
# Run 3 (Project editor) can then be authored and applied through the dashboard
# surface run 3 itself will build.
#
# Prerequisites (see README.md for full recipe):
#   - 01-analytics-project must have reached status.phase: Complete
#   - kind cluster + TIDE charts installed (see docs/INSTALL.md)
#   - tide-secrets Secret created in namespace tide-dogfood-codex
#     (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + GIT_PAT — same as analytics namespace)
#   - openai-secrets Secret created in namespace tide-dogfood-codex
#     (OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY — see README.md)
#   - per-namespace-resources applied per README.md
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: tide-dogfood-codex
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
    tideproject.k8s/sample: dogfood-codex
---
apiVersion: tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1
kind: Project
metadata:
  name: dogfood-codex-runtime
  namespace: tide-dogfood-codex
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
    tideproject.k8s/sample: dogfood-codex
spec:
  # TIDE operates against its own repo — TIDE-on-TIDE dogfood run.
  targetRepo: https://github.com/jsquirrelz/tide.git

  outcomePrompt: |
    ## Outcome

    Add a second Subagent implementation for the Codex runtime (OpenAI) and
    demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — mixed-provider waves that actually
    run, not just interface scaffolding. Concrete outcome: a TIDE operator can
    configure a Project where the planner pool runs Claude and the executor pool
    runs Codex, and TIDE dispatches a multi-phase run to completion with correct
    wave-boundary failure semantics across both providers.

    ## Architecture — locked decisions (do NOT reinvent these)

    **Implementation location: internal/subagent/codex/**
    Mirror the existing internal/subagent/anthropic/ layering exactly:
    - internal/subagent/codex/client.go — Client struct + constructor
    - internal/subagent/codex/run.go — Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
    - internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile — multi-stage build mirroring anthropic/Dockerfile
    - internal/subagent/codex/doc.go — package doc referencing D-C1 layering pattern

    All OpenAI/Codex-specific code stays behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent
    interface. No provider hard-coding in the controller. No Codex imports
    in internal/controller/ or cmd/manager/.

    **Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project).**
    Planner pool and executor pool each pin a runtime. This is the correct
    granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves real
    heterogeneity within a single run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity
    of per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen):
    - Per-task selection: large API surface, non-trivial scheduler changes.
    - Per-project selection: no mixing within a run (defeats heterogeneity goal).

    The SubagentConfig field already has Levels.{Milestone,Phase,Plan,Task}
    per-level LevelConfig overrides (api/v1alpha1/project_types.go). Wire the
    Codex image at the levels the operator wants to run Codex.

    **Credential path.**
    OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY via a K8s Secret — the established
    Secret-ref pattern from providerSecretRef. Never mount host config, never
    headless OAuth (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100 — same restriction applies
    to Codex). Chart values for the new Secret name are part of the deliverable.

    **Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics.**
    The spec's failure handling (spec section "Failure handling at wave
    boundaries") applies unchanged regardless of which provider runs a task:
    - Failed task: siblings in the same wave continue (they were declared
      independent).
    - Dependents in later waves: never dispatch.
    - Non-dependents in later waves: dispatch in strict profile.
    This contract must not change when tasks run on different providers.

    **Codex CLI facts — carry verbatim, do NOT re-research:**
    - codex exec is first-class headless: one-shot, no TTY, final message
      to stdout.
    - Flags for clean containers: --ephemeral, --skip-git-repo-check,
      --ignore-user-config.
    - --json emits a JSONL event stream.
    - --output-schema <file> constrains the final response to a JSON schema
      — use this for child-CRD emission (child-CRD format is JSON, mandated).
    - Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner needs --sandbox workspace-write
      (fine inside an already-isolated pod — mirrors the Claude runner posture).
    - API-key env auth (OPENAI_API_KEY) is the documented container path —
      no headless-OAuth problem.
    - MCP client support (STDIO + streamable HTTP) via config.toml or
      codex mcp add — available if needed for child-CRD emission.
    - Sources: developers.openai.com/codex/{noninteractive,cli/reference,
      auth,cli/features} (verified 2026-06).

    **Container image.**
    internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile must build clean. The final image
    bundles the Codex CLI binary. Image reference convention mirrors
    ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0 (replace "claude" with
    "codex" in the image name; exact tag/registry are authoring decisions).

    ## Constraints

    - All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in internal/subagent/codex/ and
      behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent interface — no provider leakage into
      the controller or cmd/manager.
    - Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves.
    - Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — reference
      by Secret name only.
    - Follow all CLAUDE.md conventions: Apache 2.0 headers, logr/zap logging,
      controller-runtime patterns.
    - Do NOT break existing tests. Confirm make test is green before
      authoring any plan as delivered.
    - Do NOT add a real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit test-env
      guard (OPENAI_API_KEY env set) — tests must pass offline.

  # Phase 2 D-C1..C4: credproxy reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from this Secret.
  # For the planner pool (Claude side of the heterogeneous run).
  # The Codex executor pool's OPENAI_API_KEY lives in a separate Secret —
  # see README.md for the openai-secrets creation step.
  providerSecretRef: tide-secrets

  # Starting cap for dogfood runs — tune before applying.
  # These are multi-phase real work runs; the cap is a safety net.
  budget:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000
    rollingWindowCapCents: 10000
    rollingWindowDuration: 24h

  # Default image + model for the planner pool (Claude).
  # Codex executor pool is wired via subagent.levels.task.image override
  # once the codex subagent image is built by this run.
  # Cheaper alternatives: model: claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5
  subagent:
    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0
    model: claude-opus-4-7

  # Phase 3 D-B6: per-project git target + creds.
  git:
    repoURL: https://github.com/jsquirrelz/tide.git
    credsSecretRef: tide-secrets

  # Human oversight at milestone and phase level; plan and task auto.
  # Fully-autonomous: change milestone and phase to auto.
  gates:
    milestone: approve
    phase: approve
    plan: auto
    task: auto
    pauseBetweenWaves: false

  planAdmission:
    fileTouchMode: strict

  maxAttemptsPerTask: 3
